page: Microsoft Azure D64alds_v7 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the Daldsv7 family, powered by the x86_64 architecture and running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The server features a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 32 physical cores and 64 vCPUs, utilizing multithreading with 2.0 threads per core. Cache specifications include 48 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low-memory ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. It is equipped with 3780 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, though it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong compute and memory capabilities. The AMD EPYC processor achieves top-tier performance in single-core stress-ng tests, PassMark CPU scores, and PassMark CPU extended instructions. Multi-core stress-ng performance and PassMark CPU physics tests rank in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is robust, with both small-block cached reads and large-block uncached reads and writes placing in the top 25%. For specialized workloads, the instance demonstrates top-tier efficiency in single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, alongside strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression.

With its high vCPU count relative to memory and large local storage capacity, the instance is optimized for compute-heavy tasks that do not require massive RAM footprints. Database operations perform in the top 10% according to PassMark benchmarks, and Redis SET operations reach the top 25%, making it highly suitable for high-throughput database hosting and web serving. It also displays top-tier performance in small and medium LLM inference tasks. Qualitatively, the server offers high compute density and substantial local storage, representing a cost-efficient profile for CPU-bound processing, though the 2.0 GB per vCPU memory allocation represents a tradeoff for memory-intensive applications.
description: Microsoft Azure D64alds_v7 is a compute-oriented virtual machine from the Daldsv7 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 64 vCPUs (32 physical cores), 128.0 GB of RAM, and 3780 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 instance provides a low-memory ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance, PassMark scores, and database operations, alongside strong multi-core and memory bandwidth results. The server lacks GPU acceleration but excels in CPU-bound workloads, including database operations, web serving, and small-to-medium LLM inference. It represents a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for high-density compute tasks, with the primary tradeoff being its limited memory-to-vCPU ratio.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64alds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 64 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor, 128 GB of RAM, and 3780 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier single-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64alds_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine with 64 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, optimized for high-density compute workloads.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring sixty-four vCPUs, substantial local storage, and top-tier single-core performance for demanding CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores.
128.0 GB of system memory providing a compute-optimized 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio.
3780 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.
Top-tier single-core CPU, PassMark, and database benchmark performance.
Strong memory bandwidth and multi-threaded decompression capabilities.
Well-suited for database operations, web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference.
